title: Mirimus COVID-19 Pool Surveillance RT-PCR Testing
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"60mnq","text":"Overview","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cd036","text":"At the start of the Pandemic in the US, New York was hit with more cases of COVID-19 than any single country in the world. Although case numbers are finally at an all-time low, the question of how to safely reopen the economy, school and businesses still remains unclear. Today, SARS-CoV-2 detection via RT-PCR methods remains the gold standard to detect active infection and recommend isolation of individuals in order to prevent further spread. Unfortunately, the cost and bottlenecks created from repeated massive testing efforts have become unsustainable. As a way to address this issue, we have devised a strategy of pooling samples and testing entire populations together as one unit. This cuts the cost of testing dramatically and still enables the detection of a susceptible group that would need to undergo individual testing.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8r66v","text":"To meet the COVID-19 testing demands, we created an organizational-based pooling strategy, whereby organizations can enroll in surveillance testing to monitor groups of people through pooled testing. By using organizational-based pooling, we avoid random pooling of samples. Instead, we pool groups of known contacts from a defined workplace, school or organization where people regularly interact. Therefore, a positive case would likely have an impact on the entire pooled population and the entire pooled population can be treated as an infected cohort until further individual testing, potentially, through a healthcare provider is recommended or offered.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bn37d","text":"Method of Pooled Testing","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":24}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9ao24","text":"For pooled surveillance testing to be effective and widely adopted, sample collection methods must also be simplified and non-invasive in order to achieve high compliance and enrollment into a surveillance program. The method of testing must also be highly sensitive to accurately detect one positive case in a large pool of negative samples. For these reasons, we developed a simple saliva collection method with a validated pooled saliva specimens using RT-PCR and an acceptable limit of detection, which will be the most sensitive diagnostic test to date. We established automated methods to pool up to 24 saliva specimens and if a positive case is detected, the pools can be further broken down to pools of 2 specimens. The remaining 2 individuals within a pool will be referred to or, potentially, offered further clinical diagnostic testing by a healthcare provider.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Place 24 tubes Eppendorf CryoStorage Vial Eppendorf Catalog #0030079485 on rack. Place pool tube in position F8. Place organization tube in position F1. Barcode scan the rack. Initiate automated decapping on the Hamilton. Hamilton LabElite DeCapper (Robot, Hamilton, 193836, https://www.hamiltoncompany.com/automated-sample-management/benchtop-devices/labelite-decapper )

Initiate automated decapping. Move decapped rack to the Integra assist. Integra Assist (Liquid Handler, Integra, 4505, https://www.integra-biosciences.com/united-states/en/pipetting-robots/assist ) Integra Voyager 1250 (Pipette, Integra, 4724, 1250, https://www.integra-biosciences.com/united-states/en/electronic-pipettes/viaflo )
Initiate the pooling automated program on the Integra Assist
Repeat pooling for each cohort
Create the Wash Buffer plate 2. Add 500 µL of Wash Buffer to each well.
Create the 80 % (v/v) ethanol plate 3. Add 1000 µL of 80 % (v/v) Ethanol, Absolute, Molecular Biology Grade Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #BP2818500 to each well.
Create the Elution plate 4. Add 50 µL of elution buffer to each well.
Scan the pool tube for QC. Initiate automated decapping.
Aspirate and mix each sample 5 times. Aspirate and dispense 200 µL from each pool tube into Sample Plate 1.
Add 200 µL of HBSS Gibco - Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #14025-092 to the negative control well.

Load the King Fisher KingFisher (RNA Extractor, Thermo Scientific, 5400610, https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/5400610?gclid=EAIaIQobChMI2-Tz36PS6wIVRb7ACh1sZACoEAAYASAAEgKwi_D_BwE&ef_id=EAIaIQobChMI2-Tz36PS6wIVRb7ACh1sZACoEAAYASAAEgKwi_D_BwE:G:s&s_kwcid=AL!3652!3!200890306469!b!!g!!#/5400610?gclid=EAIaIQobChMI2-T ) with each plate and initiate the RNA extraction. Use the standard program: MVP_2Wash_200_Flex

1) Aspirate 50 µL of 400 millimolar (mM) DTT L-1,4-Dithiothreitol Gold Biotechnology Catalog #D-830 2) Dispense 50 µL of 400 millimolar (mM) DTT into each sample 3) Mix samples 5 times, using speed setting 4) Aspirate 200 µL from each specimen 5) Dispense 200 µL into the reservoir 6) Repeat for each row until all specimens are pooled 7) Transfer the pooled specimen to the pool tube 8) Place the pool tube in a new specimen rack
Rack all pool tubes and scan again. This scan will autopopulate the qPCR template for the run.
Set up RNA extraction using the reagents provided in the MagMAX™ Viral/Pathogen II (MVP II) Nucleic Acid Isolation Kit Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #A48383

Create the sample plate 1 by combing the following from the MagMAX™ Viral/Pathogen II (MVP II) Nucleic Acid Isolation Kit Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #A48383 For each pool specimen (N), use the following recipe 1) 265 µL of Binding Solution [ 265 µL X (N+1) X 1.1] 2) 10 µL of MVPII Binding Beads [ 10 µL X (N+1) X 1.1]. Be sure to vortex well before using. 3) 5 µL of Proteinase K [ 5 µL X (N+1) X 1.1] 4) 5 µL of MS2 Phage [ 5 µL X (N+1) X 1.1] from the TaqPath™ COVID-19 Combo Kit Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #A47814 Vortext and then add 285 µL of the mixture to each sample well using the multichannel pipettes.

Transfer the purified RNA from the KingFisher Elution plate to the qPCR using the ViaFlow 96 (Liquid Handler, Integra, 6031, https://www.integra-biosciences.com/united-states/en/electronic-pipettes/viaflo-96-viaflo-384 ) Add in the positive control Twist synthetic SARS-CoV-2 RNA control Twist Bioscience Catalog #Mt007544.1

Run the program on the Mantis, to aliquot 5 µL of Master Mix to each well. Plate in triplicates for each pool sample into MicroAmp Optical 384-Well Reaction Plate with Barcode Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #4343814 . Include an additional reaction for the positive control.

Generate TaqPath COVID-19 Master Mix according to the recipe per pool sample plus 1 positive control: 1) 7.5 µL of TaqPath™ 1-Step Multiplex Master Mix (No ROX) Thermo Fisher Catalog #A28523 2) 1.5 µL of primers from the TaqPath™ COVID-19 Combo Kit Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #A47814 3) 6 µL of Water molecular-grade Fisher Scientific Catalog #BP24701 The following formula can also be use: 50% Taqpath 1-step master mix 10% Taqpath combo kit primers 40% H20

PERFORM THE NEXT STEPS IN THE CLEAN ROOM. Set-up the RT-PCR plate using the Mantis (Liquid Handler, Formulatrix, NA, https://formulatrix.com/liquid-handling-systems/mantis-liquid-handler/ ) . Select program and determine the volume required.
Label 4 Deep Well Plates and generate 1) Samples 2) Wash Buffer 3) 80 % (v/v) Ethanol 4) Elution Deep Well Plate (96 well) Thermo Fisher Catalog #10045
Seal the plate with MicroAmp™ Clear Adhesive Film Thermo Fisher Catalog #4306311
Vortex for 30 sec. Centrifuge the plate for 10 sec.

Place the plate in the QuantStudio 7 Pro (Real Time Thermocycler, Thermo Fisher, A45585, https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/A43183#/A43183 ) Use the program: 00:02:00 25 °C 1 cycle 00:10:00 53 °C 1 cycle 00:02:00 95 °C 1 cycle 45 cycles of: 00:00:03 95 °C 00:00:30 60 °C
creator: Prem Premsrirut
